businessModel.text:
Home Depot operates the world's largest home improvement retail chain, serving three primary customer types:
  
    Do-It-Yourself (DIY) Consumers: Individual homeowners purchasing tools, materials, and equipment for personal home improvement projects. This is the historical core customer base, though as the housing stock ages and DIY skills decline in younger generations, this segment is evolving.
    Pro Customers: Professional contractors, tradespeople, and construction companies purchasing building materials, tools, and supplies at scale. Home Depot has invested heavily in Pro-focused services (HD Pro Checkout, dedicated sales teams, inventory management tools) to capture this high-volume, relationship-driven segment.
    Multi-Family Housing / Rental: Property managers and landlords maintaining rental units. This is a growing segment as institutional ownership of single-family rental properties expands.
  
  Home Depot's competitive moat rests on scale advantages in procurement, an unmatched product range, and the density of its store network. The Pro customer segment is the highest-priority growth area — Pro customers spend significantly more per transaction and have higher retention rates than DIY customers.

thesisEvaluation.text:
Bull Case
      68 / 100
      Mortgage rates decline to 5.5–6.0 per cent as inflation cools, triggering a housing market recovery that drives both transaction volumes (first-time buyers entering the market) and home improvement spending. Home Depot's Pro programme drives sustained mix shift to higher-spending customers. EPS grows at 8–10 per cent annually. Shares re-rate to 26–28× as the housing recovery narrative gains traction.
      Weight: 30%
    
    
      Base Case
      55 / 100
      Housing market remains flat-to-slight-improvement through 2026. Existing home sales stabilise at current depressed levels. DIY spending stays pressured as consumers prioritise experiences over home improvement. Pro business continues to grow but cannot fully offset DIY weakness. EPS grows at 5–7 per cent as buybacks and cost discipline offset margin pressure.
      Weight: 50%
    
    
      Bear Case
      35 / 100
      Mortgage rates remain elevated above 7 per cent or rise further, causing a renewed housing market downturn. Existing home sales fall to multi-decade lows, severely impacting the Home Depot customer base. Consumers facing economic pressure reduce big-ticket discretionary home improvement spending. Lowe's survival competition intensifies, pressuring margins across the sector.
      Weight: 20%

recommendation.text:
Conviction Score: 54/100 — REDUCE
  The shares trade 20.9 per cent below the 52-week high at 23.7× earnings, providing some valuation support after the significant de-rating from the 2022 peaks. However, in an environment of subdued housing transactions and consumer spending pressure on discretionary categories, the near-term earnings trajectory lacks the momentum to drive a re-rating. Reduce at current levels; build positions on further weakness.
  
    Buy Below$290
    HOLD Zone$290–$370
    Reduce Above$370
    Stop / Sell$270

entryExit.text:
HD — Home Depot Inc (HD) — Entry / Exit Framework

Reduce / Trim: Above $344.09 — the stock is trading near or above the 52-week high. Capital should be rotated elsewhere.
Tighten or exit on any bounce above $371.07 — the risk/reward is poor at current levels.
Stop-loss (for remaining exposure): $435.29 — a close above the 52-week high would confirm the bull case is breaking down.
Target: $340.53 — partial mean reversion toward the middle of the 52-week range.
